message:
refactor(telegram): move parse-mode fallback from client to connector layer

Lift the parse-mode retry logic out of TelegramClient::send_message
and into TelegramConnector, leveraging the new SDK Formatter and
error classification system:

client.rs:
- Remove inline parse-mode retry logic from send_message (was
  catching API errors, checking is_parse_error, and retrying without
  formatting)
- Remove is_parse_error() helper function — this is now handled by
  the SDK's classify_error_message()
- send_message now simply forwards the request without retry logic

connector.rs:
- Use Formatter::render_with_fallback() to pre-render message text
  with the requested parse mode (HTML/MarkdownV2/Plain), including
  automatic plaintext fallback on malformed markup
- Use classify_error_message() / ErrorClass to determine retry
  strategy after send failures: ParseError triggers plaintext
  retry, RateLimit extracts retry_after, Transient is retryable,
  Terminal propagates immediately
- Map parse mode via FormatMode::as_parse_mode() to decouple the
  connector from Telegram-specific formatting strings

This makes the retry strategy testable, consistent across connectors,
and composable with the SDK's formatting pipeline.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
